issue_title: Require external Infinispan be of version 15 or greater
issue_body:
### Description

There are some differences in the behavior of Infinispan 15 that we depend on to ensure entries in the remote site are removed. We also don't test version 14 of Infinispan any more.

Therefore the migration guide should state that users should upgrade to ISPN 15. This also needs to be part of the [building blocks](https://www.keycloak.org/high-availability/bblocks-active-passive-sync) and [the blueprint](https://www.keycloak.org/high-availability/deploy-infinispan-kubernetes-crossdc). 

### Discussion

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-benchmark/pull/823#issuecomment-2122787262

### Motivation

The behavior of Infinispan changed across major versions and we depend on it, plus we want to keep the support matrix small.

### Details

_No response_
commit_message:
Require external Infinispan be of version 15 or greater


Signed-off-by: Ryan Emerson <remerson@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: MigrateTo25_0_0 does not complete within default transaction timeout
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

The realm migration iterates over each client regardless if it already has a 'basic' scope. It had completed at least once in the past, but won't anymore, which is leaving our clients in an inconsistent state (some have a 'basic' scope, some don't).

### Version

Nightly

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Realm migration completes within the transaction timeout. It might also work if we could skip MigrateTo25_0_0 and do the migration ourselves some other way.

### Actual behavior

Realm migration is attempted, but never completes due to Arjuna transaction reaping, which causes containers to fail to start.

> 2024-04-30 14:30:41,609 DEBUG [org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultMigrationManager] (main) Migrating older model to 24.0.3
> 2024-04-30 14:30:45,035 DEBUG [org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultMigrationManager] (main) Migrating older model to 25.0.0

### How to Reproduce?

Run Keycloak 24 against a persistent DB
Create ~2500 clients
Run nightly against same DB

issue_title: Exception while trying to run a LDAP sync with a group importer and a batch size less then the actual number of groups
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

After the first sync of users from a new LDAP federation, we encounter an exception on further updates, when the federation has a `group-ldap-mapper` configured and the batch size is configured to be less then the actual amount of groups contained in the LDAP server. I added the occuring log trace containing both the initial import as well as an update with a batch size of 1 under `Anything else?`.

We fixed the issue by increasing the batch size to be higher then the actual number of groups. The issue doesn't occur in the user sync, but as part of the group sync. If the issue occurs, the user sync isn't started.

After some recherche, it looks to me like the problem mentioned in the following Quarkus issues, which suggests an issue between agroal and hibernate:

- https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/6770
- https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/18737

I'm not sure, if this is really the cause of the issue.

### Version

24.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The batched group import of the LDAP user sync is working and doesn't throw an exception.

### Actual behavior

An exception is thrown while Keycloak is updating the second batch of groups. Sometimes, the log trace doesn't contain an exception, but multiple warnings are logged and the sync fails nontheless.

### How to Reproduce?

I created a Gist containing both a docker-compose file with a Open-LDAP instance and a LDAP-UI application, a LDIF file containing 50 groups with 1 user each and a realm export with a user-federation using the Open-LDAP and a `group-ldap-mapper`.

Using this setup, the issue can be reproduced as follows:

- If necessary, delete all groups and remove all imported users
- Go to the `ldap` user federation and trigger a user import by selecting `Sync all users` under `Actions`
  - The user import works as intended and no exception occurs
- Go to the `ldap` user federation and trigger a user import by selecting `Sync all users` under `Actions`
  - A log message by agroal occurs after the first batch, that `Closing open connection(s) prior to commit`
  - An exception is thrown, that the `Connection is closed`

### Anything else?

issue_title: Missing Cache-Control header when "response_type" parameter is missing in login request
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

When a login request is made without `response_type` parameter, the `checker.checkResponseType()` function in `AuthorizationEndpoint.java` at line 158 returns a `redirectErrorToClient` with `QueryRedirectUriBuilder` as the response mode. 
In this scenario, the "invalid request" error is redirected to client without including a "Cache-Control" header. Consequently, when using CloudFront with default cache settings, since the "Cache-Control" header is absent, the response is cached. Subsequent requests to the login endpoint then receive the same "invalid request" response. 
This presents a problem because the login endpoint is dynamic content and needs to respond in real-time. Similar to token refresh or user info endpoints, it should include a `no-cache` annotation to prevent caching.

### Version

21.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When a login request is made without `response_type` parameter,  `redirectErrorToClient` with `QueryRedirectUriBuilder` as the response mode should redirect "invalid request" error and a cache-control header with no-cache value should be added to response headers.

### Actual behavior

When a login request is made without `response_type` parameter,  `redirectErrorToClient` with `QueryRedirectUriBuilder` as the response mode redirect "invalid request" error to client and no cache-control header. so when using CloudFront with default cache settings, Subsequent requests to the login endpoint then receive the same "invalid request" response.

### How to Reproduce?

Add caching policy to cloud front for keycloak server. (Default TTL: Set to your preferred value (e.g., 600 seconds).)
make a (code grant) login request with preferred client_id without response_type parameter. 
then make login request with response_type parameter provided. 

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Increased GC overhead in the continuous performance tests after G1GC compiler change
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Increased GC overhead in the continuous performance tests after the default garbage collector is set to G1GC.

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

We want the GC behavior to be consistent and not have skewed resource utilization for better predictability of the application.


### Actual behavior

In some cases the CPU utilization of one of the pods gets skewed in terms of utilization.
While the JVM reports 2.5% overhead, the overall CPU usage of the pod doubles.

Below are the resource utilization from a scheduled performance test:

CPU utilization:
![run1-cpu](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/3415105/f02b736a-cb42-42e6-8416-3df1397c451d)

JVM GC Overhead*:
![run1-jvm-gc-overhead](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/3415105/63e3f08b-8c49-479a-94ce-f6d4f463bbfa)


```
* The jvm_gc_overhead_percent metric refers to the percentage of time the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) spends in garbage collection (GC) as part of the total time available.
Garbage collection is a process by which the JVM reclaims memory by identifying and disposing of objects that are no longer in use, freeing up memory for new object allocations.
```

### How to Reproduce?

Refer to the https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-benchmark/actions/workflows/rosa-scaling-benchmark.yml continuous performance tests.

### Anything else?

We further analyzed the GC logs and found these JVM options are making eager heap space changes and might be counter productive after the garbage collector is set to `G1GC` as default.

```
-XX:AdaptiveSizePolicyWeight=90 -XX:MinHeapFreeRatio=10 -XX:MaxHeapFreeRatio=20 -XX:GCTimeRatio=4
```

When dealing with the [support for JDK 21](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/28518), I've encountered an issue with the x509 Subject Alternative Name (SAN) User Principal Name (`otherName`) in certificates. 

It seems the attribute is not properly handled when using JDK 21. It works properly with JDK 17.

To check the SAN props of certificate with `openssl x509 -noout -text -in test-user-san@localhost.cert.pem`:

```

                email:test-user-altmail@localhost, IP Address:192.168.7.1, DNS:www.example-test.com, URI:http://www.example-test.com, othername: 1.2.3.4::my_test_user, othername: UPN::test_upn_name@localhost
```

In normal situations, the page should redirect to the `login-x509-info.ftl`, but with the JDK 21 and the specific test case, the page is redirected to the login screen and requires authentication.

@mposolda @rmartinc Could you please check it?

issue_title: CI for JS not running all the tasks
issue_body:
Several of the tasks running under the JavaScript CI are not being executed correctly. This is because the argument being passed into the [PNPM filter](https://pnpm.io/filtering) are incorrect, which causes none of the projects in the workspace to be matched.

These filters should be corrected and changed so that they fail when no match is found (see [`--fail-if-no-match`](https://pnpm.io/filtering#--fail-if-no-match)).

issue_title: Admin UI slow performance loading 600+ realms
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

Whenever I try to open the admin UI and click the realms dropdown, it takes so long to load realms drop list.

After inspecting the dev tool requests, it appears that this request is the root cause:
`auth/admin/realms/master/ui-ext/realms/names?`

The request first takes approximately 3 mins to return with code 200 but the admin UI is still loading
![Screenshot (499)](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/39639769/683c82c6-6913-44ca-a3e9-a45a735349f4)

After 3 additional minutes, the response is fully loaded to the UI
![Screenshot (500)](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/39639769/e90a74e5-c69c-4479-8f68-9dce8eb98df4)

---------------------------------------------------
I believe this issue was fixed in previous versions of Keycloak. I've searched the currently open bugs to see if there's any similar bugs, but didn't find any.
Possibly a similar bug to this [20453](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/20453)
---------------------------------------------------

### Version

24.0.x

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Realms dropdown list loads faster.

### Actual behavior

Realms dropdown takes approximately 6 mins with current setup of 600+ realms.

### How to Reproduce?

- A Keycloak environment with huge number of realms, my case is 600+ realms
- Open admin UI through this link: `https://{host}/auth/admin/master/console/`
- Click on the realms dropdown list

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Using {application.session.host} in backchannel logout url prevents from saving client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

If a client is configured with a parameterized backchannel logout url (such as `http://{application.session.host}:1234/something/sso-logout`), the Admin Console fails in saving the client.

Actually the issue is not in the frontend but in the validation rules on backend side. The `PUT /realms/{realm}/clients/{client}` returns an HTTP 500. I think it's due to a `URI uri = new URI(url);` in the url validation method 

### Version

22.0.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Having the backchannel logout url validation rule be less restrictive so the {application.session.host} parameter feature can be used without interfering with any other client configuration to come.

### Actual behavior

* Cannot configure a dynamic backchannel URL from the Admin Console
* If a dynamic backchannel URL was configured, the Client configuration cannot be changed anymore.

### How to Reproduce?

* Set the client backchannel logout url to `http://{application.session.host}:1234/something/sso-logout` (we do it from a dedicated SPI but can be updated directly in database)
* Edit the client in the Admin Console
* Save the client => HTTP 500 - unknown error

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: All roles are populated as inherited roles if a single role is added to a dedicated client scope
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the scope tab of a dedicated client scope, if "Full Scope Allowed" is disabled and a role is added, all the roles in the realm are added as implied roles and are visible once the "Hide inherited roles" box is unchecked.

### Version

24.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

  A SAML client is created. Under the *Client Scopes* tab, the dedicated scope is selected. Within the dedicated scope, the *scope* tab is selected. The "Full scope allowed" toggle is set to off, and a role is assigned. When "Hide inherited roles" is unchecked, only the assigned role and default roles should be visible.

### Actual behavior

A SAML client is created. Under the *Client Scopes* tab, the dedicated scope is selected. Within the dedicated scope, the *scope* tab is selected. The "Full scope allowed" toggle is set to off, and a role is assigned. When "Hide inherited roles" is unchecked, every role in the realm is visible.

### How to Reproduce?

Create several roles within a realm. Create a SAML client in the realm. Select the client scopes tab of the SAML client, select the client's dedicated scope, and select the scope tab. Turn off "Full scope allowed" and add a single role to the scope. Uncheck "Hide inherited roles" to see all the realm's roles added.

### Anything else?

This issue appears to be similar to [issue19982](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19982) and may be related.

issue_title: Update RFC reference in subject: Likely typo RFC2553 -> RFC2253, Consider RFC4514
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The baloon help of "Alllow Regex Pattern Comparison" in Credential tab in the client setting mentions "The Subject DN can be in the RFC2553 or ...".

RFC2553 is likely a typo for RFC2253. Furthermore, RFC2253 is already obsolete, so the correct reference should be RFC4514.

 Says RFC4515

Says RFC2253

1. create a client with Client authentication=ON
2. click Credentials tab
3. change Client Authenticator to X509 Certificate
4. click the "?" next to Allow regex pattern comparison.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Remove sources folder before invoking JakartaTransformer
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

There a few sub-projects that execute the `JakartaTransformer` to transform from old `javax` to new `jakarta` classes. They just transform other project classes to be valid for the new jakarta specification. The problem is that the transformer class just overrides files and, if one class is removed in the original project, the file remains in the target folder if it was transformed previously. That can generate compilation issues in the working project.

So this issue is to try to remove the source folder before executing the transformation.

### Version

999

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Delete the target folder before executing the transformation avoiding the compilation issues.

### Actual behavior

The deleted files remain in the directory.

### How to Reproduce?

This was seen after merging PR for issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/29912

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Remove the transformed output directory before executing JakartaTransformer
Closes #30086

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Updating client policies in JSON editor is buggy. Attempt to update global client policies should throw the error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

There are issues with client policies JSON editor similar to the issues, which were in client profiles JSON editor #27184. It can be seen when going in admin console to some realm -> tab `Realm Settings` -> tab `Client policies` -> tab `JSON editor` . It looks it is combination of server and UI issues.


1) When trying to save invalid JSON like for instance add manually this content:
```
[ sdfsdf ]
```

There is a bit confusing error message `Provided JSON is incorrect: Unexpected token { in JSON`


2) Same confusing error message when trying to update valid JSON, but incorrect schema. For instance when doing typo in word `enabled` like this:

```
[
  {
    "name": "fff",
    "description": "",
    "enableed": true,
    "conditions": [
    ],
    "profiles": [     
    ]
  }
]
```


3) When I successfully update client policy like for example this:

```
[
  {
    "name": "fff",
    "description": "",
    "enabled": true,
    "conditions": [],
    "profiles": []
  }
]
```

it will successfully update the policy and then it would add additional attribute `"global": false` to the JSON. This is correct so far. But when I wanted to save it another time, it is again impossible with same error like in case (1) and (2). It seems UI not able to handle the `global` attributes, which should be probably handled same way as done for client profiles.


4) When updating global policies in JSON editor, the update appears to be done, but in reality it is not done, but it is silently reverted. This is confusing as it would be better to notify administrator right away that updating global policies is not supported. This is very similar to the issue with client profiles described in #27184 . 

For "global" client policies to appear in the UI, it is currently needed to start server with `./kc.sh start-dev --spi-security-profile-default-name=strict-security-profile` as for the default security profile, there are not any global client policies configured.


### Version

main, 24.0.X as well

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Sensible error messages in case that it is not possible to update client policies. Notify admin that it is not possible to update global client policies instead of silently reverting updates done by admin to global client policies

### Actual behavior

Error messages not very useful in case that it is not possible to update client policies. When admin tries to update global client policies, it appears that it was successfully updated even if they were not updated because updating global policies is not allowed

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

The issue is partially regression. I think that point (3) is a regression of the change done for https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/27189
commit_message:
Updating client policies in JSON editor is buggy. Attempt to update global client policies should throw the error
closes #30102

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

issue_title: Workaround for cache initialization failure discovered in #30120
issue_body:
### Description

We discovered that the configuration default values are not properly set when the Infinispan is started in a different thread. The workaround suggested is to create the Infinispan configuration from the main thread and only start Infinispan asynchronously. 

Issue: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/30120
commit_message:
Workaround for cache initialization failure discovered in #30120

Create Infinispan configuration in the main thread to have access to the
default option values.

Fixes #30130

Signed-off-by: Pedro Ruivo <pruivo@redhat.com>
